Director/PDMR Shareholding
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, announces that that on 9 March 2026, Jeremy Warner Allen, Non-Executive Chairman of the Company, has transferred, via sale and purchase (the "Transfer"), Ordinary Shares into his ISA. Mr Allen sold 3,827,494 Ordinary Shares at a price of 0.313 pence and purchased 3,827,494 Ordinary Shares at a price of 0.314 pence to be held in his ISA.
Following the transfer, Mr Allen's beneficial shareholding remains unchanged at 3,827,994 Ordinary Shares, representing
1.07
% of the Company's issued share capital.

latest.title: Update on Ongoing Legal Proceedings
latest.content:
18 Dec 2025 15:29
RNS Number : 1776M
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
18 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Update
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, provides
an update on the ongoing legal proceedings relating to its subsidiary, Karanja Terminal & Logistics Pvt. Ltd.
A civil appeal was listed before the Supreme Court of India earlier today, in which the appellant sought to set aside the interim order previously granted in MPL's favour by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) on 10 December 2025.
Following submissions from all parties, the Supreme Court did not pass any adverse order against the Company and directed the NCLAT to hear the matter on 14 January 2026, and, if possible, to dispose of the appeal on that date.
Importantly, the interim order dated 10 December 2025 remains in force.
The Company will continue to pursue all legal remedies available to protect its interests and will provide further updates as appropriate.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker)
Stephen Keys
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

latest.title: Update
latest.content:
17 Dec 2025 09:37
RNS Number : 9350L
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
17 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Update
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, provides an update on the legal position and
confirms that it continues to pursue vigorously all legal remedies to safeguard its wholly owned strategic asset, Karanja Terminal & Logistics Pvt. Ltd. ("KTPL"), located in Navi Mumbai, India.
The Company recently secured interim protection from the Insolvency Appellate Tribunal, which restrains the solicitation of expressions of interest from third parties, thereby preserving the status quo while legal proceedings are ongoing.
MPL has also formally offered to redeem the full outstanding debt relating to the asset, exercising its lawful right to settlement. The debt had previously been assigned to Prudent ARC, a transaction which MPL is contesting and which forms part of ongoing legal proceedings.
Prudent ARC has since appealed against the interim order to the Supreme Court of India. The matter is scheduled to be heard on Thursday, 18 December 2025.
MPL remains confident in the strength of its legal position and is committed to defending shareholder value while ensuring that due process is followed.
The Company continues to enjoy the support of its major global stakeholders.
Commenting on the development, Pav Bakhshi, Managing Director of Mercantile Ports & Logistics, said:
"We have made a credible and lawful offer to redeem the outstanding debt in full. Karanja is a strategic infrastructure asset that rightfully belongs to MPL, and we will continue to pursue all legal avenues to protect the Company's interests and those of its shareholders."
Further announcements will be made as developments unfold.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker)
Stephen Keys
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

latest.title: Result of General Meeting
latest.content:
15 Dec 2025 11:17
RNS Number : 6006L
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
15 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Result of General Meeting
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, is pleased to announce that further to the Company's announcement of 19 November 2025, at the General Meeting held earlier today the Resolutions proposed were duly passed by shareholders.
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for the cancellation of the Redemption Shares and admission of the Subscription Shares to trading on AIM. It is expected that admission of the Subscription Shares will occur and that dealings will commence at 8.00 a.m. on 16 December 2025.
Unless otherwise defined herein, capitalised terms in this announcement have the same meaning as in the shareholder circular published on 19 November 2025.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

(Nomad and Broker)

company_30s.what_they_do: Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited runs port and logistics operations across the UK, handling container traffic, warehousing and supply‑chain services for shipping lines and freight forwarders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global container trade is worth trillions of dollars. MPL supplies a critical link in that chain by operating key terminal sites and offering integrated freight solutions, giving it a foothold in a market that is slowly expanding as e‑commerce and supply‑chain resilience demand more efficient logistics.
company_30s.current_state: MPL is in a period of restructuring. Recent filings show a new loan facility, a pending court decision on a high‑profile dispute, and a director‑shareholding change. The company is still generating revenue but faces cash‑flow pressure and legal uncertainty, trading at a very low price that reflects the market’s doubts.

rns_narrative: MPL is juggling legal challenges – a court order is still pending and the company has updated on the status of that proceeding. The court ruling could either free the company or lock it into a costly settlement.

Financially, the firm has secured a loan facility to shore up liquidity, but interim results still show modest earnings and a need for further capital. Governance changes, such as a director’s shareholding shift, signal an attempt to bring fresh leadership into the mix.
The company is also preparing for a general meeting and related‑party transactions that could inject capital or restructure existing obligations.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet uncertainty – the community is waiting for the court’s verdict while staying wary of cash‑flow issues.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter. The few positive posts are speculative about a favorable ruling but lack substance.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on legal risk, potential cash burn, and the fact that the company’s price remains near its low end. They fear the court could impose further penalties or the loan could be denied.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from cautious optimism in early 2025 to heightened caution as the court date approaches. Engagement remains low, signalling that most traders are still on the sidelines.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – there is no current public interest, which means any news that surfaces could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The court decision on 6 Oct 2025 – a favorable ruling could unlock assets and reduce legal costs.
Disbursement of the new loan facility – a cash injection that would improve liquidity.
Release of the next interim results – if earnings beat expectations, it would signal operational improvement.
Any new shipping contracts or terminal agreements announced in the next 60 days – these would demonstrate revenue growth potential.
A surge in trading volume around any of the above events – a volume spike is a classic sign that the market is taking notice.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the price for a break above the most recent low (the 52‑week low). A sustained uptick coupled with a volume jump over 150% of the average would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis. Also keep an eye on the court’s press release and any changes to the loan terms.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early to mid‑stage of a deep crash recovery. The APEX score indicates tight compression, so a small catalyst could trigger a big move. Entry should only be considered after a clear confirmation of a reversal – the court ruling or loan disbursement – to avoid the risk of a false break.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the system has not flagged a crash bottom and the company still faces significant legal and liquidity risks.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the court clears MPL’s path and the loan is funded, the stock could recover to the midpoint of its 52‑week range. Historical data for RED‑color signals (deep crashes) shows average returns of 160%+ when a bounce occurs. A rebound to the mid‑range would therefore represent a 100–200% upside over the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: A negative court decision, denial of the loan, or a continued cash‑flow crunch could push the stock further down, potentially to a liquidation level. The worst realistic outcome is a decline to the bottom of the 52‑week range, which would still be a sizable loss relative to the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 3–4 times the current price if the stock reaches the mid‑range, while the maximum loss is limited to the current price level. The asymmetry is therefore favourable for a contrarian who is comfortable with a long‑term hold and can tolerate a temporary decline.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the court decision or loan funding, allocating no more than 5% of your portfolio. Place a stop just below the recent 52‑week low to limit loss. Use a trailing stop that locks in gains once the price moves above the 50‑day moving average.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position (or simply stay out) until volume spikes above 150% of the 20‑day average and the price closes above the 52‑week low. This confirms the reversal and reduces the risk of a false breakout.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings, but remain alert for any sudden regulatory changes.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MPL is a deep‑crashed logistics player facing legal uncertainty; a court win or loan funding could trigger a sizable rebound, but the risk of further decline remains high.
